overview: This plant is identified as a Zamia Fern (Bowenia spectabilis), a type of cycad native to Queensland, Australia. It is known for its glossy, fern-like fronds that can grow up to 1 meter in length, emerging from a subterranean stem. Often grown as an ornamental plant, it thrives in deep shade and wet lowland forest environments. iNaturalist +4 5 sites Zamia Fern (Bowenia spectabilis) · iNaturalist Zamia Fern (Bowenia spectabilis) · iNaturalist. More. Cycads Class Cycadopsida. Cycads Order Cycadales. Zamia Family Family Zamiac...
